name: Page header

variants.name: top_level
variants.description: First level page. No back button, no status tags.

variants.name: second_level
variants.description: Detail/sub page. Has back button and optionally status tags.

actions.note: Max 3 buttons. For layout S: max 1 button optionally opening option list.

back_button_label.note: Second level only. Label must match the headline of the parent top-level page.

sticky.note: When sticky, page header collapses on scroll: back button disappears, headline shrinks, spacing reduces, subline hides.

usage_rules.detail: Layout templates already include a page header. Do not add a second one.
No more than 3 action buttons in the page header. For responsive layout S, only 1 button shown — additional actions go in an option list.

usage_rules.rule: Status tags on second level only
usage_rules.detail: Status tags are only allowed on second_level page headers.

usage_rules.rule: Back button label matches parent headline
usage_rules.detail: The back button label must match the headline of the corresponding top-level page.
Headline and description must use sentence case.

confidence_blockers: If ticket places page header inside a layout template that already contains one, flag as duplication.
If ticket adds more than 3 action buttons, flag — excess actions go in option list.
If ticket adds status tags to a top_level page header, flag as usage_rule violation.

embedding_text: Page header component. Page title screen header at top of content area. Variants: top level first level page, second level detail page with back button. Headline subline status tags actions. Max 3 actions 1 on small layout. Back button label matches parent page headline. Sticky collapses on scroll. Sentence case.
